title: If I Only Touch the Hem
subtitle: A woman who has bled twelve years reaches, trembling, for the fringe of His garment

stillness: Be still, and come close from behind, as she did.
She did not dare approach His face for the press of the crowd.
Approach Him now in the quiet, with the devotion of faith.
Carry to Him the shame you bear for your own fault.
gospel: Behold, a ruler of the synagogue, named Jairus, came to Jesus.
He fell down at His feet and worshipped Him.
He pleaded for his only daughter, twelve years old:
"Lord, my daughter is even now dead.
But come, and lay Thy hand upon her, and she shall live."
And Jesus, rising up, followed him, and His disciples.
And behold, a woman, who had an issue of blood twelve years,
who had spent all her substance on physicians, and could not be healed,
came up behind Him and touched the hem of His garment.
For she said within herself, believing firmly:
"If I shall touch only His garment, I shall be healed of my infirmity."
And touching, at once she was healed of her plague.
And Jesus, knowing the power that had gone out from Him, said: "Who hath touched me?"
The disciples said: "The crowds throng and press Thee."
But He said: "Someone hath touched me; for I know that power hath gone out from me."
The woman, seeing that she was not hidden, came trembling,
and fell down before His feet, and declared before all the people
the cause of her touching, and how she was healed.
And Jesus, turning to her, said: "Be of good heart, daughter;
thy faith hath made thee whole. Go in peace."
behold: Behold attentively, as if you were present.
Behold the Lord Jesus, who has no doorkeepers, no guards to thrust back the press of the crowd.
So the woman comes freely, while the throng crushes against Him.
Behold her shame: she comes from behind, and from the back,
for she blushes at her foul and unclean affliction,
and under the Law she is counted unclean.
Behold her humility: she judges herself unworthy
to touch the feet of the Lord, or the fullness of His garment,
and so she reaches only for the hem.
Behold how she does not touch by the body's touch alone,
but with the touch of the devotion of faith.
She has despaired of the physicians, on whom she poured out all she had,
and now she understands that the heavenly Physician is here.
Behold her come forward, fearing in heart and trembling in body
before the majesty of the Godhead, whose power she has felt within herself.
consider: Consider what the body of Christ is, that showed such power even in its hem.
She, says Rabanus, received her medicine out of a wound.
What then ought we to hope, who do not only touch the Lord's Body,
but make it, and take it, and eat it?
And woe to those for whom the very medicine is turned into a wound!
She drew the cure not from the garment's power,
but from the power of Him whose the garment is.
By three things is all salvation acquired: by word, by faith, by deed.
She came, and coming she spoke; she spoke, and speaking she touched; she touched, and touching received her cure.
He asks "Who hath touched me?" not from ignorance,
but that the healed woman may confess, and her faith be made plain.
The crowds pressed against Him, yet they did not touch Him as she touched Him,
for they pressed without her devotion and her faith.
Mark this: He did not say, "I have healed thee," but "thy faith":
shunning all ostentation, He exalts the merit of faith,
teaching us in our good acts to seek not our own praise, but God's.
He calls her daughter by reason of her faith,
for the faith of Christ bestows the adoption of God.
And "Go in peace" He says, sending her to the end of all good things,
for in peace God dwells:
that she may know she is healed not in body only,
but changed also from the causes of her bodily suffering, which are her sins.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, I adore the feet of Thy mercy and Thy truth.
Most loving One, I beseech Thee:
heal by the touch of Thy grace my soul,
bleeding and stained with manifold sins.
Raise it from the death of an evil and hidden will and purpose,
and restore me to God and Thy Father,
who hast adopted me as a son among Thy co-heirs.
Remember not, good Lord, Thy justice against Thy sinner,
nor Thy wrath against Thy guilty one;
but be mindful of Thy kindness toward Thy creature,
and of Thy compassion toward Thy wretched one,

latinJewel.latin: Si tetigero tantum vestimentum ejus, salva ab infirmitate mea ero.
latinJewel.english: If I shall touch only His garment, I shall be healed of my infirmity.
